segments.text: David said,
Is there anyone still left of Saul's family so I can be kind to him for Jonathan's sake?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: There was a man called Ziba who used to be a servant of Saul's family. They called for him to come to David, and the king asked him,
Are you Ziba?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Yes, I am your servant,
segments.speaker: Ziba

segments.text: he replied.
The king asked him,
Is there anyone still left of Saul's family so I can be kind to him as I promised before God?
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: There's still one of Jonathan's sons, who is lame in both feet,
segments.speaker: Ziba

segments.text: Ziba replied.
Where is he?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: asked the king.
He's in the town of Lo-debar, living in the home of Machir, son of Ammiel,
segments.speaker: Ziba

segments.text: Ziba replied.
So King David had him brought from Machir's home.
When Mephibosheth,  son of Jonathan, son of Saul, came to David, he bowed facedown to the ground in respect. Then David said,
Welcome, Mephibosheth.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I am your servant,
segments.speaker: Mephibosheth

segments.text: he replied.
Do not be afraid,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: said David,
for I will truly be kind to you for the sake of your father Jonathan. I will return to you all the land owned by your grandfather Saul, and you will always eat at my table.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Mephibosheth bowed down and said,
Who am I, your servant, that you should pay any attention to a dead dog like me?
segments.speaker: Mephibosheth bowed down and

segments.text: Then the king called for Saul's servant Ziba and told him,
I have given to your master's grandson everything that belonged to Saul and his family.
segments.speaker: Mephibosheth bowed down and

segments.text: You and your sons and workers are to farm the ground for him and bring in the produce, so that your master's grandson will have food to eat. But Mephibosheth, your master's grandson, will always eat at my table.
segments.speaker: Mephibosheth bowed down and

segments.text: Ziba had fifteen sons and twenty workers.
Ziba replied to the king,
My lord the king, your servant will do everything that you have commanded.
segments.speaker: Ziba

segments.text: So Mephibosheth ate at David's table like one of the king's sons.
Mephibosheth had a young son named Mica. All the people who lived in Ziba's house became Mephibosheth's servants.
But Mephibosheth lived in Jerusalem, because he always ate at the king's table. He was lame in both feet.
